blocks.body:
Most productivity apps make you feel busy before you've done anything. Notifications, badges, sync indicators, premium tiers. They dress up task management as if complexity were a feature.

Atelier disagrees.

A kanban board is a simple thing: a few columns, some cards, a way to move them around. That's the whole job. Atelier does that job and stops.

items.title: Local-first, always
items.body: Your work is yours. It lives on your device, in a plain JSON file you can read and back up yourself. No server knows what you're working on.

items.title: No configuration required
items.body: Open the app and it works. Three columns, one project, ready to go. If you need more projects or a different accent colour, the options are there — but you don't have to touch them.

items.title: Calm interface
items.body: No animations that demand attention. No modals stacked on modals. The board is quiet so you can think.

left.title: Built with Tauri
left.body: Atelier runs as a native macOS app via Tauri 2 — a thin Rust shell wrapping a React frontend. You get a proper .app bundle, title bar overlay, and system-level feel without Electron's overhead.

right.title: Fonts bundled in
right.body: Fraunces and Schibsted Grotesk ship inside the app via Fontsource. Atelier needs no network connection to render correctly — open it in a cabin with no Wi-Fi and it looks exactly the same.

blocks.quote: A tool should fit the hand. Atelier is built to disappear into the work.
blocks.author: Atelier
